title: Mouth bite animation on mouse hover

requirements.summary: Add interactive mouth component with hover-triggered bite animation that cycles through smile, open, and bitten states
acceptanceCriteria: When the mouse cursor moves over the mouth area, the mouth visibly opens (shows interior/teeth) for approximately 150–200ms.
After the open phase, the mouth animates to a closed/bitten position and remains there.
The closed/bitten mouth does not revert to the original smile on its own.
Moving the mouse over the closed mouth again triggers the full open-bite-closed sequence again.
The open and bite animation uses smooth SVG path transitions or rapid state changes that feel snappy and playful.
The mouse hit area for the mouth is large enough to be reliably triggered without pixel-perfect cursor placement.

file_operations.description: Replace the static smile path (lines 342–349) with an interactive Mouth component that implements the bite animation. Add a React state variable `mouthState` with three values: 'smile' (initial curved smile), 'open' (wide open mouth showing interior/teeth), and 'bitten' (closed/flat or slight frown). Create a transparent SVG hit area (ellipse or rect centered around y=255, covering the mouth region) with an onMouseEnter handler. On mouse-enter, trigger a state transition: smile → open (hold for 150–200ms) → bitten. When in 'bitten' state and mouse-enter fires again, repeat the open → bitten sequence. Use SVG <path> elements with distinct d attributes for each state, and optionally add interior elements (e.g., a white ellipse for teeth/interior) visible only in the 'open' state. Keep the mouth component self-contained within App.tsx alongside the existing Eye component pattern. Ensure the existing eye, blink, and pupil-tracking logic (lines 1–266) remains untouched.
